reasoning: 39-point AdjEM gap is insurmountable. Duke's archetype matches 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) and 2015 Kentucky (F4) — elite tournament profiles. Foster's absence doesn't matter against a 16-seed with a first-tournament coach. Duke's 10-0 L10 and ACC championship momentum are commanding.

Ohio State has the AdjEM edge (8.4), but Brandon Noel has been out since January and Chatman is day-to-day. Jake Diebler is a first-tournament coach facing Jamie Dixon's 14-15 tournament record across 16 appearances. TCU's 8-2 L10 momentum and Dixon's March pedigree overcome the efficiency gap. TCU's archetypes (2021 UCLA F4, 2022 Michigan S16) project deeper runs.

Rick Pitino's 55-22 tournament record is the best in the field. St. John's AdjEM edge (14.6) is well outside upset territory, and their archetypes (2023 UConn Champion, 2018 Duke E8) project elite tournament performance. Big East champions on a 9-1 L10 run with an elite defense (AdjD 94.1).

Bill Self's 57-24 tournament record (2 titles) against a first-tournament coach makes this a coaching massacre. The 16.3-point AdjEM gap puts this well outside upset range. Darryn Peterson's absence lowers Kansas's ceiling but not enough against Cal Baptist.

Louisville's 10.5-point AdjEM gap is decisive, but South Florida's 9-1 L10 as AAC champions demands respect. Mikel Brown Jr.'s back injury is significant. Louisville's archetypes (2016 Villanova Champion) are elite, but the efficiency gap just barely holds Louisville here.

Tom Izzo's 59-26 tournament record with 8 Final Fours is the most dominant coaching resume in the field. The 21.3-point AdjEM gap makes this automatic at the 3-14 line. NDSU lost their PG for the season.

UCLA's 8.4-point AdjEM advantage is offset by devastating injuries: Bilodeau (leading scorer, out/limited) and Dent (calf, limited). UCF's Big 12-tested roster and archetypes (2022 Michigan S16, 2024 Colorado S16) project mid-tournament viability against a depleted Bruins squad.

Dan Hurley's 15-5 tournament record with back-to-back titles is the gold standard. UConn's 30.2-point AdjEM gap and championship DNA make this automatic. UConn's archetypes (2024 Purdue, 2015 Wisconsin) project deep runs.

The largest AdjEM gap in the field (43.8). Florida's archetypes are breathtaking: 2025 Florida (Champion), 2024 UConn (Champion), 2015 Kentucky (F4). Todd Golden's 6-2 tournament record is efficient. Most lopsided matchup in the bracket.

Iowa actually leads in AdjEM (21.9 vs 19.6), and Clemson is devastated: Carter Welling (torn ACL) and Dillon Hunter (broken hand) both out. Iowa's 4-6 L10 COLD momentum is concerning, but Clemson's injuries are more damaging.

Vanderbilt's 17.1-point AdjEM gap is firmly outside upset territory. Archetypes (2016 Villanova Champion, 2024 Purdue Runner-Up) project elite tournament potential. McNeese's perfect 10-0 L10 is impressive but the conference strength gap is enormous.
Nebraska's 22.1-point AdjEM gap and elite defense (AdjD 93.5) make this comfortable. Archetypes (2010 Duke Champion, 2021 Houston F4) project deep runs. Troy's Sun Belt offense will be strangled.

UNC lost Caleb Wilson (broken thumb) — leading scorer and projected top-5 pick. VCU is 9-1 L10 as A-10 champions running HAVOC defense. UNC's 5-5 L10 COLD momentum signals a team in freefall. VCU's archetypes (2022 Michigan S16, 2024 Colorado S16) project mid-tournament viability. Injury + momentum overwhelm the seed advantage.

Illinois' 32.6-point AdjEM gap is insurmountable. Second-best offense in the tournament (AdjO 131.9). Archetypes match 2024 Purdue (Runner-Up) and 2015 Duke (Champion). Penn's Ethan Roberts dealing with a concussion further weakens an overmatched squad.

Saint Mary's holds the 3.3-point AdjEM edge with Randy Bennett's 7-11 tournament record and 25 years of program continuity. Texas A&M's McMillan is in his first year (0-1 tourney). Saint Mary's 8-2 L10 HOT momentum vs A&M's 6-4 neutral. Bennett's grinding system neutralizes A&M's tempo.
Kelvin Sampson's 31-21 tournament record (4 FFs) is elite. Houston's 30.3-point AdjEM gap and nation-leading defense make this automatic against a first-tournament coach from the Big Sky.

Arizona's 41.6-point AdjEM gap is second-largest in the bracket. Archetypes: 2025 Florida (Champion), 2012 Kentucky (Champion). Big 12 championship momentum (9-1 L10) against LIU's first-ever tournament coach.

Utah State leads in AdjEM (20.9 vs 19.1) with a 9-1 L10 HOT streak as MWC double champions. Villanova's Kevin Willard has a concerning 4-7 tournament record. Utah State's momentum and efficiency edge make them the better pick.

Wisconsin's 17.4-point AdjEM gap is well outside upset territory. Archetypes (2016 Oklahoma F4, 2016 Villanova Champion) project deep runs. High Point's 30-4 record earns respect but the conference gap is enormous.

Calipari's 57-22 tournament record (6 FFs, 1 title) is among the best ever. 21.2-point AdjEM advantage and SEC championship momentum. Acuff Jr. returned for SEC tourney. Hawaii's cross-Pacific travel adds fatigue.

BYU's 2.8-point AdjEM edge is slim. Sean Miller has deeper tournament resume (22-13 vs 2-1), but Texas didn't qualify for SEC tournament (5-5 L10). BYU's marginal efficiency advantage barely carries the day.

Mark Few's 44-25 tournament record against a first-tournament CUSA coach. 25-point AdjEM gap and WCC championship momentum (9-1 L10 HOT). Huff and Warley injuries lower ceiling but 3-14 gap is too wide.

Miami's 2.7-point AdjEM edge is modest. 8-2 L10 HOT momentum vs Missouri's 6-4 neutral is the tiebreaker. Both coaches are relatively inexperienced. In a coin-flip 7-10 game, the hotter team gets the nod.
Purdue's nation-best offense (AdjO 133.3) meets Queens' worst defense in the bracket (AdjD 117.1). 35.5-point AdjEM gap. Big Ten championship momentum. First-ever D1 tournament for Queens.

38.3-point AdjEM gap with archetypes (2012 Kentucky Champion, 2025 Florida Champion) projecting champion-caliber. Second-best defense in the country (AdjD 91.0). Dusty May's 6-3 tournament record (1 FF).

2.2-point AdjEM gap slightly favors Georgia, but Saint Louis is 8-2 L10 HOT with a 28-5 record. Archetypes (2022 Iowa State S16, 2024 Colorado S16) project viability. Georgia at 6-4 neutral with White's .500 tournament record. The hotter team wins the coin flip.
15.7-point AdjEM advantage well outside upset territory. Archetypes (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2016 Villanova Champion) project deep runs. Big 12 vs MAC efficiency and physicality gap is too wide.
Nate Oats' 11-6 tournament record and Alabama's fastest tempo (73.1) overwhelm Hofstra's first-tournament coach. 18.5-point AdjEM gap. Archetypes (2016 Oklahoma F4, 2022 UNC Runner-Up) project deep.

Tennessee's 7.9-point AdjEM edge and elite defense (AdjD 95.5) control this matchup. Rick Barnes' 28-26 tournament record across 21 appearances provides massive experience edge. Ament's return is a positive sign.

23-point AdjEM gap and 8-2 L10 HOT momentum as ACC runner-up. Sanchez is a first-tournament coach — normally a red flag — but the talent gap against Wright State is too wide. Archetypes (2024 Purdue, 2018 Michigan) project deep runs.

Santa Clara's AdjEM (21.3) exceeds Kentucky's (19.9). Kentucky's injuries are devastating: Quaintance OUT, Lowe OUT for season, Moreno questionable. Archetypes (2016 Oklahoma F4) project strong tournament performance. The data demands this pick.
35.3-point AdjEM gap with elite defense (AdjD 92.6). Otzelberger's 9-5 tournament record against a first-tournament coach. Archetypes (2024 Purdue Runner-Up, 2025 Houston Runner-Up) project deep.
